meta.project: OpenPaws — AI Animal Welfare Compliance Platform
meta.scope: All routes from NextJS Route Map. No pages removed. Navigation consolidated. Flow restructured for clarity and professionalism.

spacing.note: All spacing must be multiples of 4px. Prefer 8px base unit.

borders.note: Use borders over shadows for element separation. No box-shadows on cards.

shadows.note: Only used for focus states. No decorative shadows.

status_badge.usage: Inline on document rows, finding cards, report headers

risk_badge.anatomy: colored left stripe (3px) + background fill + text
risk_badge.usage: Finding cards, analysis rows, review queue items

confidence_badge.usage: AI-generated findings only, always paired with 'AI Draft' indicator

stat_card.note: No shadows. Border only. Clean separation.

anatomy: metadata chips: source type · jurisdiction · species (body_sm, grey_500)
trigger passage block (JetBrains Mono, 12px, grey_700, background #F8FAFC, border 1px #E5E7EB, padding 8px 12px)
metadata row: jurisdiction chip · confidence badge · AI Draft indicator
action row: Accept · Dismiss · Flag · Comment (right-aligned buttons)

buttons.placement_rule: Primary action right-aligned. Destructive actions always last and separated by gap. Never float buttons.

tables.text_alignment: Left for text. Right for numbers, percentages, timestamps.
tables.note: Simple borders only. No cell backgrounds except header. No zebra striping.

empty_states.alignment: centered vertically and horizontally in container
empty_states.tone: Directive, not apologetic. Tell the user exactly what to do next.

shell.type: Fixed sidebar + fixed topbar + scrollable main content
shell.note: App shell is fixed. Content area scrolls independently.

logo_area.content: OpenPaws wordmark (DM Serif Display, 18px) + paws icon

navigation_groups.note: Primary task-oriented navigation. These are the 5 things a reviewer does daily.

items.label: Documents
items.description: Document library and search. Includes /search and /search/saved as in-page tabs on this route.
items.note: Search is accessible via /search but surfaced as a persistent search bar in the Documents sidebar panel — not as a separate sidebar nav item.

items.label: Review Queue
items.description: Human sign-off queue. Primary governance action point.

items.label: Analysis
items.note: Upload → Analysis is a single flow. Clicking 'Analysis' brings you to /analysis/new which has an inline upload step. /uploads and /uploads/:batchId are intermediate pipeline steps within this flow, not separate nav entries.

navigation_groups.note: Reference data. Less frequently accessed. Visually separated by a 24px gap and section label.

items.label: Knowledge Base
items.description: Imported precedent, guidance, and rule corpus.

navigation_groups.note: Contextual/specialty workflows. Accessible but not primary. Collapsible group — collapsed by default for new users.

items.label: Watchlists
items.description: Saved monitoring lists for recurring document sets.

items.label: Settings
items.note: /design is accessible from Settings as a tab (Design System / Appearance)

items.label: Admin
items.visibility: Only shown if user role is Admin or Org Admin

removed_from_sidebar.rationale: Search is a behaviour, not a destination. Surfaced as a persistent global search bar in the topbar (⌘K) and as a filter/search panel within Documents.
Uploads are a pipeline step within Analysis. The flow is: Documents → Start Analysis → upload prompt. /uploads/:batchId is a progress/status page reachable from Analysis, not a top-level destination.

removed_from_sidebar.item: Contracts / Gap Audits / Watchlists at top level
removed_from_sidebar.rationale: Moved to Operations collapsible group. Reduces cognitive load for reviewers who primarily use Core Workflow.

removed_from_sidebar.item: Admin and Settings at top-level main nav
removed_from_sidebar.rationale: Moved to sidebar footer. These are configuration, not workflow.

topbar.layout: Three zones: [left: breadcrumb] [center: global search] [right: status + actions]

left_zone.content: Breadcrumb trail (body_sm, grey_500 › grey_800 for current)

center_zone.content: Global search input — ⌘K shortcut. Placeholder: 'Search documents, rules, reports…'
center_zone.activates: Command palette / search overlay — searches /search and /search/saved routes

content: Governance gate badge (e.g. 'Human Review Required' — coral background when active)
User avatar button → dropdown: Profile · Switch org · Sign out

topbar.note: Remove the floating pill tabs (Contracts, Gap Audits, Chat, Admin, Settings) from topbar. These caused confusion — they overlapped with sidebar navigation. Topbar is status + search only.

dashboard.layout: Single column header + 2-column body (2/3 main + 1/3 sidebar panel)

header.title: Dashboard
header.subtitle: Operational overview for ingestion, analysis, review, and governance-safe export readiness.

cards.label: Indexed Documents
cards.description: Across inspections, policies, contracts, and guidance.

cards.label: Pending Review
cards.description: Candidate concerns waiting on reviewer action.

cards.label: Knowledge Coverage
cards.description: Mapped rule and precedent corpus coverage.

sections.title: Recent documents and runs
sections.empty_state: No documents indexed yet. Upload your first document to begin.

sections.title: Active analysis runs
sections.layout: Document row list (analysis variant — shows progress state)
sections.empty_state: No active analyses. Start an analysis to queue decision-support review.

items.label: Search corpus
items.description: Run hybrid retrieval across documents and rules.

items.label: Start analysis
items.description: Queue a document for decision-support review.

items.label: Add rule
items.description: Review imported or draft rule entries.

items.label: View reports
items.description: Check internal and export-gated report packets.

guardrail_banner.placement: Bottom of page, full width, above footer
guardrail_banner.content: Findings remain candidate concerns or possible gaps until a human reviewer confirms next steps.

documents.layout: Left filter panel (240px) + main list area + optional right detail panel (slide-in)
documents.note: This page consolidates /search, /search/saved, /documents, and /documents/:documentId. Search and filter live inside this page.

header.title: Documents
header.note: 'Saved searches' tab corresponds to /search/saved route. No separate sidebar nav item needed.

applied_filters_bar.shows: Active filter chips with remove (×) button each

document_list.pagination: Infinite scroll with 'Load more' trigger at 80% scroll
document_list.empty_state: No documents match your filters. Try adjusting the criteria or upload new documents.

document_detail.layout: Right-side slide-in panel (640px wide) OR full-page route depending on viewport
panel_sections: Document header: title + metadata + status badge
Chain of custody: origin · ingest date · source · jurisdiction (JetBrains Mono for IDs)
Tabs: Overview | Analysis runs | Findings | Chat
Note: /documents/:documentId/chat is the Chat tab within this detail view

analysis.layout: Stepped wizard — 3 steps: Upload → Configure → Review
analysis.note: /uploads and /uploads/:batchId are steps within this wizard flow, not separate destinations. The URL updates as the user progresses.

steps.title: Upload documents
steps.content: Drag-and-drop upload zone + batch name input + file list with status. Accepts PDF, DOCX, TXT.

steps.title: Configure analysis
steps.content: Select rulebook(s) to run against. Choose jurisdiction. Set analysis type (standard / gap audit). Confirm document list.

steps.title: Analysis in progress
steps.content: Live status panel. Per-document progress bars. Log feed (JetBrains Mono). Estimated completion time.

header.content: Run ID (mono) · Date · Rulebook used · Status badge · Document name

findings_list.grouping: By severity: Critical → High → Medium → Low
findings_list.filter_bar: Filter by severity, status (accepted/dismissed/pending), rule ID

finding_detail_panel.shows: Full finding_card + related rule text (DM Serif Display) + source passage (JetBrains Mono) + action history thread

analysis_review.note: This is the AI-assisted pre-review step before findings go to the human Review Queue.
analysis_review.layout: Full-width table of findings with bulk-action toolbar
analysis_review.confirm_step: Modal confirmation before sending to Review Queue: 'Send X findings to human review queue?'

header.title: Review Queue
header.filter_bar: Filter by: All · Pending · In review · Complete · My items

queue_list.layout: Scrollable list of finding_card items (compact variant)
queue_list.sort: By severity (critical first) then by date submitted
queue_list.group_by: Optionally group by document or analysis run

content: Evidence chain: document → analysis run → flag ID
Reviewer action panel: Accept · Dismiss · Edit finding · Add comment · Sign off

sign_off_action.label: Sign off finding
sign_off_action.confirmation_modal: Are you sure you want to sign off this finding? This action will be logged and cannot be undone.
sign_off_action.generates: Audit log entry + triggers report generation if all findings resolved

governance_note.content: This finding is an AI-assisted draft. Your sign-off is required before it can appear in any exported report.

header.content: Report title · Source · Analysis run ID (mono) · Status badge · Governance state badge

body_sections: Audit trail (JetBrains Mono table: action · actor · timestamp · ID)
Export controls (locked until all findings signed off)

export_gate.locked_state: Export button disabled, shows: 'X findings pending human review before export.'
export_gate.unlocked_state: Export button enabled — primary teal. Options: PDF · DOCX · CSV

rulebooks.layout: Left list + right detail — same pattern as Documents

rulebook_detail.overview_tab: Metadata: name, jurisdiction, species, description, import source, version chain
rulebook_detail.rules_tab: Paginated rules table with search. Each row: rule ID (mono) · text (serif) · severity · status

test_tab.content: Paste or upload a test document. Run analysis against this rulebook only. Preview findings.

versions_tab.content: Version history table. Diff view between versions. Restore previous version action.

rule_detail.content: Full rule text (DM Serif Display) · source document · jurisdiction · linked analysis runs · edit action

import_flow.content: Upload knowledge source (PDF / URL / API) → parse preview → confirm import

review_flow.content: Table of pending imported rules awaiting approval. Approve / reject per row.

contracts.layout: Left filter + document row list (same pattern as Documents)
contracts.note: Contracts follow the same Upload → Analysis → Review → Report pipeline as standard documents. The workflow is identical but filtered to contract type. Internal routes /contracts/:analysisRunId and related /review/:flagId and /reports/:reportId links are contextually tagged as contract-type in metadata.

gap_audits.note: Gap Audits are a structured analysis type. The flow is: New audit → select rulebook → configure scope → run → review findings → report. Same wizard pattern as Analysis. /gap-audits/:analysisRunId, /review/:flagId (gap audit context), and /reports/:reportId (gap audit context) follow the same UX as standard analysis detail.

watchlists.layout: Card grid (3 columns) of watchlist summaries

watchlist_detail.layout: Left list of watched documents/rules + right metadata panel

admin.layout: Left sub-nav (within admin) + main content area
admin.access: Admin and Org Admin roles only. Redirect non-admins to /dashboard.

evaluations_detail.layout: Full-page evaluation run report with metrics table and chart

sub_nav_items.label: Appearance
sub_nav_items.note: This is where /design route content lives. Not a separate top-level page.

design_route.note: The /design route is retained but internally redirects into Settings > Appearance. No separate sidebar nav entry.

navigation_flow_summary.explanation: The core reviewer journey. Upload (via Analysis wizard) → automated analysis → human review → signed-off report.
secondary_workflows: Contract review: /contracts → [same as primary but filtered to contracts]
Gap auditing: /gap-audits → [same wizard as analysis but structured differently]

contextual_actions.description: Actions that cross workflow boundaries should be surfaced as contextual links, not sidebar nav changes.

breadcrumbs.typography: body_sm, grey_400 for parents, grey_800 for current

dashboard_no_documents.heading: No documents indexed yet.
dashboard_no_documents.body: Upload your first document to begin compliance review.

review_queue_empty.heading: No findings pending review.
review_queue_empty.body: All current findings have been reviewed. Run a new analysis or check completed reports.

documents_no_results.heading: No documents match your filters.
documents_no_results.body: Adjust the filters or upload new documents.

loading_states.page_load: Skeleton screens — block placeholders matching the anatomy of the target component. No spinners.
loading_states.analysis_progress: Progress bars per document + log feed in JetBrains Mono
loading_states.inline_data: Shimmer skeleton of the data row/card
loading_states.note: Never show a full-page loading spinner. Always show structural skeleton.

error_states.page_level_error: Alert banner at top of content area: 1px solid #FECACA, background #FFF3F0, left border 3px solid #D92D20
error_states.empty_api_error: Empty state with error message and retry action

confirmation_modals.usage: Destructive or irreversible actions only: sign-off, bulk dismiss, delete, export
anatomy: Confirmation input for highly destructive actions (type 'DELETE' to confirm)

governance_guardrail_pattern.description: Any AI-generated content must carry a permanent visual indicator until human sign-off is confirmed.
governance_guardrail_pattern.visual: Gold left border (3px, #D19900) + 'AI-assisted draft · Human review pending' label (label typography, gold color)
governance_guardrail_pattern.post_signoff: Replaced with green left border + 'Reviewed by [name] · [timestamp]' label
governance_guardrail_pattern.note: This pattern must appear on every finding_card, every report section, and any exported draft.

accessibility.contrast_ratios: All text meets WCAG AA minimum (4.5:1 for normal, 3:1 for large). Primary teal #006C67 on white = 6.2:1 (passes AA).
accessibility.keyboard_navigation: All interactive elements focusable. Focus ring: 2px solid #006C67, 3px offset. Tab order follows visual reading order.
accessibility.screen_reader: All icons have aria-label or paired visible text. Status badges use aria-label for semantic meaning. Tables use proper thead/th/scope.
accessibility.motion: All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion: no transitions above 150ms when reduced motion is requested.
accessibility.form_labels: All inputs have associated label elements. Error messages linked via aria-describedby.

responsive_behaviour.sidebar_mobile: Collapses to bottom tab bar (5 tabs: Dashboard, Documents, Review, Analysis, More). 'More' expands a sheet with remaining items.
responsive_behaviour.sidebar_tablet: Collapses to icon-only (56px). Hover/focus expands to full width.
responsive_behaviour.main_content_tablet: Single column. Right detail panels become full-page routes.
responsive_behaviour.note: Primary use case is desktop 1280px+ for professional compliance work. Mobile is secondary but functional.

dark_mode.trigger: System preference (prefers-color-scheme: dark) + manual toggle in Settings > Appearance
dark_mode.note: All other design tokens remain semantically consistent. Only background/surface/border/text and primary swap. Risk colors retain their semantic meaning.

next_js.routing: App Router. All sidebar items map to top-level app/(authenticated)/[route]/page.tsx.
next_js.layout: Single RootLayout with AppShell component (sidebar + topbar). Pages render in <main> slot.
route_groups: (app) — all authenticated routes — wrapped in AppShell

component_architecture.page_components: Each page has its own page.tsx + layout-specific sub-components
component_architecture.state: Server components for data. Client components for interactive elements. Use React Context for AppShell state (sidebar open/close, active route).

fonts.loading: next/font/google for DM Serif Display, Space Grotesk, JetBrains Mono. All loaded with display: swap.
